subject: [COMMON][MARIADB] Fix backup job
commit_message:
[COMMON][MARIADB] Fix backup job

The backup job stopped working after upgrade to bitnami images.
Mariabackup was not designed to work remotely, it is supposed to run
on the database server. Because of this we need to mount the data pvc
into the backup job pod. It will however connect to the database daemon
using a hostname, so we need to connect to the first replica in the
cluster. Also had to set readOnlyRootFilesystem=false and add emptyDir
volumes to solve various permission issues.

Issue-ID: OOM-2932
Signed-off-by: Jozsef Csongvai <jozsef.csongvai@bell.ca>
Change-Id: I776903f9ec541f8dc5818b2ba4c1292226ec2bc6

Patch Set 1:

as the PVC is in RWO, doesn't it make it unable to be added to the cronjob?

Patch Set 2:

Gitlab controller: Deployment put on queue, number 1 on the queue.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> as the PVC is in RWO, doesn't it make it unable to be added to the cronjob?

It mounts fine and the job is able to read but not write, which is good because the job should not interfere with the data

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > as the PVC is in RWO, doesn't it make it unable to be added to the cronjob?
> 
> It mounts fine and the job is able to read but not write, which is good because the job should not interfere with the data

I went back to read the docs on accessmode, and I now understand your concern. In my testing, I verified that the PVC was RWO, and that the backup job ran on a different node than the database pod. Seems like this works because my test environment uses nfs storage:

https://stackoverflow.com/questions/63412552/why-readwriteonce-is-working-on-different-nodes

"A volume’s AccessModes are descriptors of the volume’s capabilities. They are not enforced constraints. The storage provider is responsible for runtime errors resulting from invalid use of the resource."

"For example, NFS offers ReadWriteOnce access mode. You must mark the claims as read-only if you want to use the volume’s ROX capability. Errors in the provider show up at runtime as mount errors."

looks like readonly defaults to false

  data:
    Type:       PersistentVolumeClaim (a reference to a PersistentVolumeClaim in the same namespace)
    ClaimName:  master-mariadb-galera-master-mariadb-galera-0
    ReadOnly:   false

This behavior must have been the same even before the upgrade to bitnami images. Should I add a comment in values.yaml regarding this?
